Notice Of Motion And Motion To Intervene; To Set Aside Default Judgment Against Defendant Insured; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Dec-24-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Ilona Brusil, Esq. 415-608-9224 for Plaintiff.
Yaelle Gurel, Esq. Smith, Koyama & Costello, 510-267-7272 for Defendant.
Court reporter: Jamie Ply, CSR #13323, 714-316-3757, Reported.

ARGUED. Defendant CSAA contests the tentative ruling. Ms. Gurel argues. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

OTHER CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANGE's Motion And Motion To Intervene; To Set Aside Default Judgment Against Defendant Insured.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Proposed Intervenor CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANGE's Motion To Intervene; To Set Aside Default Judgment Against Defendant Insured is DENIED.

The court considers plaintiff's untimely opposition since it is basically the same opposition that CSAA has previously received for the prior motions and CSAA's reply addresses the merits. (See Carlton v. Quint (2000) 77 Cal.App.4th 690, 697 [party may waive notice defect by addressing the merits].)

Code of Civil Procedure section 387(d)(1) allows for intervention "upon timely application." CSAA's application is untimely. The accident in this case occurred approximately ten years ago. CSAA was aware of this lawsuit shortly after plaintiff filed it in 2016, refused to accept service, and denied the claim because it believed it to be time-barred. (Brusil Decl., pars. 5-7.) CSAA could have moved earlier to protect its interests and prejudice exists since a key witness (insured Vu) is now unlocatable.

In any event, even if intervention is warranted, there is no basis to vacate the default/default judgment. The only competent evidence shows that the insured was properly served. (Proof of Service (12/4/19).) It appears that the default was entered as the result of the insured's and/or CSAA's derelictions. CSAA admits that relief is unavailable under Code of Civil Procedure section 473 and there is no basis for equitable relief. Based on this record, the policy in favor of the finality of judgments prevails. "[D]uring the period when relief under section 473 is available, there is a strong public policy in favor of granting relief and allowing the requesting party his or her day in court. Beyond this period there is a strong public policy in favor of the finality of judgments and only in exceptional circumstances should relief be granted." (In re Marriage of Stevenot (1984) 154 Cal.App.3d 1051, 1071.)

Lastly, there is no basis for an open-ended stay of execution of the judgment.

Order is signed after the hearing.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Jamie Ply, CSR #13323, 714-316-3757, Reported. (301/JMQ)

MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-26-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Yaelle E. Gurel, Esq., SMITH, KOYAMA & COSTELLO (510) 267-7272 Yaelle.Gurel@csaa.com
Attorneys For Defendant, Dung Vu
No other appearance are made.

Counsel for Plaintiff is not present at the time the matter was. Motion is discussed, not ARGUED. Counsel for Plaintiff Ilona Brusil, Esq., appeared in Court at the end of the hearing and after the motion was heard.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DUNG VU's MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT.

The "motion for order to set aside default (CCP 473)" is denied without prejudice. This action regards a 2014 traffic accident. Defendant driver Dung Vu was served with summons in November 2019. Default and default judgment were entered against Vu on December 15, 2023. Vu's insurer says it "has been unable to contact him," but "can intervene" and move to lift the default judgment against Vu. (Memo. 2:9-10, 18-19.) However, the insurer has not moved to intervene.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-19-2023 Off Calendar

Declaration In Support Of Application For Default
Off Calendar Dec-15-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, being set for hearing on Plaintiff's request for entry of default judgment.

Dept. 505; Judge Maria Evangelista; Clerk N Webb; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 9:18 am and concluding at 9:33 am.

Plaintiff Manuel Quintana is present via Zoom Video Conference represented by Ilona Brusil, Esq.
Defendant Dung Vu is not present.

Plaintiff presents summary of facts, claims and an offer of proof.

The matter is submitted.

By the statements made by Plaintiff and its counsel, documents filed, and evidence presented, the Court finds that Plaintiff has provided sufficient evidence proving each element of the complaint.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Manuel Quintana, and against Defendant Dung Vu, as follows:

1. Damages: $40,000.00
2. Prejudgment Interest: $37,642.80
3. Costs: $1,591.00

Attorney fees waived.

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $79,233.80
